summary: Markets surged to fresh all-time highs on Wednesday as President Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely, relieving geopolitical pressure that had weighed on risk assets. The S&P 500 gained nearly 1% to a new record while the Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.6% to its own all-time high, with semiconductor stocks notching their 16th consecutive session of gains—the longest winning streak ever. Tesla reported better-than-expected Q1 earnings after the bell, beating on EPS despite a revenue miss, as the company expanded its Robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston. Bitcoin rallied above $78,000 following the ceasefire extension and a massive $2.54 billion purchase by MicroStrategy, while Ethereum climbed past $2,390. Looking ahead, investors are laser-focused on next week's FOMC decision on April 29 and mega-cap tech earnings from Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta, which report after that same close. With oil still elevated near $100, Q1 GDP and PCE inflation data due April 30, and Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing revealing political tensions over the Powell investigation, markets face a critical test of whether tech can justify its premium valuations and the Fed can maintain its hawkish stance.

signals.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely
signals.desc: President Trump's announcement of indefinite ceasefire extension removed geopolitical overhang, triggering risk-on rallies across equities and crypto with S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting fresh records.

signals.title: Semiconductors Hit 16-Day Win Streak Record
signals.desc: Chipmakers extended their winning streak to 16 consecutive sessions—the longest ever—as AI infrastructure demand remains robust ahead of mega-cap tech earnings next week.

signals.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $78K on MicroStrategy Buy
signals.desc: Bitcoin rallied to $78,300 after MicroStrategy disclosed a $2.54 billion BTC purchase (absorbing 3x April miner supply) and ceasefire extension boosted risk appetite, testing key $79-80K resistance.

signals.title: FOMC Meeting April 29 with No Dot Plot
signals.desc: Fed holds rates at 3.50-3.75% as expected, but with no SEP projections, Powell's language on inflation vs. growth trade-off will carry outsized weight for rate cut timing into H2 2026.

signals.title: Tesla Beats EPS but Robotaxi Revenue Uncertainty Looms
signals.desc: Tesla reported Q1 EPS of $0.41 vs. $0.35 expected and expanded Robotaxi to Dallas/Houston, but revenue missed at $22.39B and concerns over multi-trillion dollar chipmaking capex persist.

verdict: Risk-on momentum is strong but faces major tests next week with FOMC, mega-cap earnings, and key macro data—hold above support or face sharp reversal.
